title: Slice: Get First Half
description: Given an array, return a new array containing only the first half of the elements. If the array length is odd, include the middle element in the first half.
whatShouldHappen: Calculate the middle index using Math.ceil(arr.length / 2).
Use the .slice() method to extract elements from index 0 to the middle index.
Return the new array.
patternExplanation: .slice(start, end) creates a shallow copy of a portion of an array without modifying the original.

title: Splice: Remove Every Other
description: Given an array, remove every second element *in-place* and return the modified array. Start removing from the element at index 1.
whatShouldHappen: Loop through the array starting from index 1.
Use .splice(i, 1) to remove the element at the current index.
Adjust the loop counter because splicing shortens the array.
Return the original (now modified) array.
patternExplanation: .splice() modifies the original array. When removing elements in a loop, be careful as indices shift.

title: Map: Price Tags
description: Given an array of numbers, return a new array where each number is converted to a string formatted as a price (e.g., 10 becomes '$10.00').
whatShouldHappen: Use the .map() method to iterate over the array.
For each number, use .toFixed(2) to ensure two decimal places.
Prepend a '$' sign.
Return the new array.
patternExplanation: .map() creates a new array by applying a function to every element of the original array.

title: Filter: Long Words
description: Given an array of strings and a minimum length, return a new array containing only the strings that have at least that many characters.
whatShouldHappen: Use the .filter() method on the input array.
Check if the length of the current string is greater than or equal to the minimum length.
Return the filtered array.
patternExplanation: .filter() creates a new array with all elements that pass the test implemented by the provided function.

title: Reduce: Inventory Total
description: Given an array of objects representing items in an inventory (e.g., { name: 'apple', count: 10 }), return the total count of all items.
whatShouldHappen: Use the .reduce() method to accumulate the total count.
Initialize the accumulator to 0.
In each iteration, add the 'count' property of the current item to the accumulator.
Return the final sum.
patternExplanation: .reduce() executes a reducer function on each element, resulting in a single output value.

title: Find: First Negative
description: Given an array of numbers, return the first negative number found. If no negative number exists, return undefined.
whatShouldHappen: Use the .find() method on the array.
Define a condition that checks if a number is less than 0.
Return the result of the .find() call.
patternExplanation: .find() returns the value of the first element in the provided array that satisfies the provided testing function.

title: Sort: Numeric Order
description: Given an array of numbers, return a new array with the numbers sorted in ascending numeric order. Be careful: the default .sort() treats elements as strings!
whatShouldHappen: Create a copy of the array using spread syntax or .slice() to avoid mutating the original.
Call .sort() on the copy.
Provide a comparison function (a, b) => a - b to handle numeric sorting correctly.
Return the sorted copy.
patternExplanation: Default .sort() converts elements to strings. Numeric sorting requires a comparator function.

title: Every: All Positive
description: Given an array of numbers, return true if every number in the array is greater than zero, and false otherwise.
whatShouldHappen: Use the .every() method on the array.
The callback should return true if the current number > 0.
Return the boolean result.
patternExplanation: .every() tests whether all elements in the array pass the test implemented by the provided function.

title: Some: Has Even Number
description: Given an array of integers, return true if at least one number in the array is even, and false otherwise.
whatShouldHappen: Use the .some() method on the array.
The callback should return true if num % 2 === 0.
Return the boolean result.
patternExplanation: .some() tests whether at least one element in the array passes the test implemented by the provided function.

title: Splice: Insert at Index
description: Given an array, an index, and an element, insert the element into the array at the specified index *in-place*. Return the modified array.
whatShouldHappen: Use .splice(index, 0, element) to insert without removing any elements.
Return the modified array.
patternExplanation: .splice() can be used to insert elements by setting the delete count (second argument) to 0.
